# Investments and Asset Management (IAM-SG)

> Lecture notes for the INSEAD MBA *Investments and Asset Management* course (Singapore, 2026), by Ben Charoenwong. Fourteen sessions across four themes — asset management as a human problem, as a science, as an art, and the state of the art (ESG, alternatives, AI). Math is in LaTeX; symbols are documented in the glossary. License: see repository.

The deployed website is at <https://bencharoenwong.github.io/iam_mba/>. The full source is on GitHub at <https://github.com/bencharoenwong/iam_mba>. For LLM ingestion, prefer `llms-full.txt` (the entire textbook concatenated as a single markdown file, regenerated on every build).

## Core resources

- [Full text (concatenated)](llms-full.txt): every chapter in one markdown file — the canonical LLM-ingestion artifact.
- [Symbol glossary](SYMBOL_GLOSSARY.md): standard mathematical notation for returns, risk, portfolio construction, factor models, and option pricing. Read first before interpreting equations.
- [Preface and instructor bio](index.html): course overview, learning objectives, prerequisites, revision history.

## Theme 1 — Investing as a Human Problem

- [Ch 01 — The Asset Management Industry](ch01-asset-management-industry.html): structure of the global $112T industry, principal–agent problems, retail investor performance evidence.
- [Ch 02 — Financial Planning](ch02-financial-planning.html): household finance, goal-based investing, lifecycle hypothesis, opportunity cost.
- [Ch 03 — Market Efficiency](ch03-market-efficiency.html): EMH, information sets, behavioural anomalies, joint-hypothesis problem.
- [Ch 04 — Democratization of Finance](ch04-democratization.html): fintech, robo-advisors, retail access to institutional tools.

## Theme 2 — Investing as a Science

- [Ch 05 — Diversification](ch05-diversification.html): risk reduction through imperfectly correlated assets, idiosyncratic vs. systematic risk.
- [Ch 06 — Modern Portfolio Theory](ch06-modern-portfolio-theory.html): mean-variance optimization, efficient frontier, capital allocation line.
- [Ch 07 — Active vs. Passive](ch07-active-vs-passive.html): the arithmetic of active management, indexation, cost-efficiency tradeoff.
- [Ch 08 — Factor Investing](ch08-factor-investing.html): value, size, momentum, quality, low-volatility — risk premia and implementation.

## Theme 3 — Investing as an Art

- [Ch 09 — Active Strategies](ch09-active-strategies.html): alpha-seeking, long–short equity, alternative risk premia.
- [Ch 10 — Security Selection](ch10-security-selection.html): bottom-up fundamental analysis, financial-statement modelling, valuation.
- [Ch 11 — Risk Management](ch11-risk-management.html): VaR, expected shortfall, stress tests, drawdown control.

## Theme 4 — State of the Art

- [Ch 12 — ESG and Sustainability](ch12-esg-sustainability.html): integrating environmental, social, governance factors into the investment process.
- [Ch 13 — Alternative Investments](ch13-alternative-investments.html): private equity, venture capital, real estate, commodities.
- [Ch 14 — AI in Asset Management](ch14-ai-asset-management.html): machine learning, NLP, LLMs in research and trading.

## Optional

- [References](references.html): full bibliography.
- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/bencharoenwong/iam_mba): Quarto sources, build pipeline, issue tracker.
